initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of anterior myocardial infarction?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose anterior myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of spiked pacemaker artifacts preceding the QRS complex
Presence of PR-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of scooped ST-segment depression in precordial leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST elevation in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V1, V2, V3, and V4? Note that ST elevation is defined as an elevation of the J-point greater than 0.1mV (1mm) in these leads.

ecg_grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST elevation? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with ST elevation? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of anterior myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
